question: [File: 'File Name: src/SystemState.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: setUSDSGasCompensation / closeTrove] Can an unprivileged BORROWER under PRECONDITIONS (trove opened when USDSGasCompensation=10e18, timelock later decreases USDSGasCompensation to 5e18) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (openTrove with 10e18 gas comp minted to GasPool → timelock executes setUSDSGasCompensation(5e18) → closeTrove burns debt.sub(5e18) from borrower and 5e18 from GasPool), violating INVARIANT (total USDS burned on close must equal total USDS minted on open including gas compensation), causing scoped impact: GasPool retains 5e18 USDS permanently, inflating USDS supply relative to protocol debt accounting, and future liquidators claim this surplus as gas compensation they did not earn? Local proof idea: deploy unit test with two-phase USDSGasCompensation change, assert usdsToken.totalSupply() == activePool.getUSDSDebt() + defaultPool.getUSDSDebt() after close, expect invariant broken.

[File: 'File Name: src/SystemState.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: setUSDSGasCompensation / _sendGasCompensation / _liquidateNormalMode] Can an unprivileged LIQUIDATOR under PRECONDITIONS (troves opened when USDSGasCompensation=10e18, timelock increases USDSGasCompensation to 20e18 before liquidation) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (openTrove mints 10e18 to GasPool → price drops → timelock executes setUSDSGasCompensation(20e18) → liquidator calls liquidate → _sendGasCompensation sends 20e18 USDS to liquidator from GasPool), violating INVARIANT (liquidator USDS gas compensation must not exceed the amount minted to GasPool at trove open), causing scoped impact: liquidator receives 20e18 USDS but GasPool only holds 10e18, draining USDS from other depositors or causing returnFromPool to revert, creating bad debt? Local proof idea: unit test with GasPool balance check before and after liquidation, assert gasPool.usdsBalance() >= USDSGasCompensation sent to liquidator.

[File: 'File Name: src/SystemState.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: setUSDSGasCompensation / _redeemCollateralFromTrove] Can an unprivileged REDEEMER under PRECONDITIONS (trove opened with USDSGasCompensation=10e18 so stored debt=netDebt+10e18, timelock increases USDSGasCompensation to value > stored debt) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (openTrove → timelock setUSDSGasCompensation(largeValue) → redeemCollateral → _redeemCollateralFromTrove computes debt.sub(usdsGasCompensation) which underflows), violating INVARIANT (redemption must not revert due to parameter change on existing troves), causing scoped impact: all redemptions against affected troves revert, trapping redeemer USDS and blocking protocol redemption path, or if underflow is exploitable, USDSLot is computed incorrectly allowing over-redemption of BNB? Local proof idea: fuzz test with USDSGasCompensation values from 0 to type(uint256).max, assert _redeemCollateralFromTrove never underflows on existing troves.

[File: 'File Name: src/SystemState.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: setUSDSGasCompensation / _getCompositeDebt / openTrove] Can an unprivileged BORROWER under PRECONDITIONS (USDSGasCompensation set to 0 via timelock, no lower bound enforced in _setUSDSGasCompensation) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (timelock executes setUSDSGasCompensation(0) → borrower calls openTrove with _USDSAmount=minNetDebt → compositeDebt=netDebt+0=netDebt → ICR computed on smaller compositeDebt → borrower passes ICR check with less collateral than intended), violating INVARIANT (compositeDebt must always include gas compensation buffer so ICR check is conservative), causing scoped impact: borrower opens trove with collateral insufficient to cover gas compensation, creating undercollateralized position and bad debt at liquidation? Local proof idea: unit test with USDSGasCompensation=0, compare ICR at open vs ICR with original gas comp, assert collateral covers full composite debt.

[File: 'File Name: src/SystemState.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: setBorrowingFeeFloor / _requireValidMaxFeePercentage / _triggerBorrowingFee] Can an unprivileged BORROWER under PRECONDITIONS (borrowingFeeFloor set to 0 via timelock, oracleRate=0 due to fresh Pyth price with zero deviation, baseRate=0 after decay) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (timelock executes setBorrowingFeeFloor(0) → borrower calls openTrove with maxFeePercentage=0 → _requireValidMaxFeePercentage passes since 0>=0 → getBorrowingFee returns 0 → borrower mints USDS with zero fee), violating INVARIANT (borrowing fee must always be >= borrowingFeeFloor and > 0 in normal mode), causing scoped impact: borrower extracts USDS debt with zero fee, depriving SABLE stakers of fee revenue and allowing fee-free USDS minting? Local proof idea: unit test with borrowingFeeFloor=0 and oracleRate=0, assert USDSBorrowingFeePaid event amount > 0.

[File: 'File Name: src/SystemState.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: setRedemptionFeeFloor / requireValidMaxFeePercentage / redeemCollateral] Can an unprivileged REDEEMER under PRECONDITIONS (redemptionFeeFloor set to 0 via timelock, baseRate=0 after full decay, oracleRate=0 from fresh Pyth price) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (timelock executes setRedemptionFeeFloor(0) → redeemer calls redeemCollateral with maxFeePercentage=0 → requireValidMaxFeePercentage passes → _getRedemptionFee returns 0 → redeemer receives full BNB with zero fee), violating INVARIANT (redemption fee must always be >= redemptionFeeFloor and protect against arbitrage), causing scoped impact: redeemer extracts BNB collateral at 1:1 USDS with zero fee, draining protocol fee revenue and enabling risk-free arbitrage? Local proof idea: unit test with redemptionFeeFloor=0 and baseRate=0, assert BNBFee in Redemption event > 0.

[File: 'File Name: src/SystemState.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: setMCR / _liquidateNormalMode / _getTotalsFromBatchLiquidate_NormalMode] Can an unprivileged LIQUIDATOR under PRECONDITIONS (MCR lowered via timelock from 110% to 101%, troves with ICR between 101% and 110% now become liquidatable) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (timelock executes setMCR(1.01e18) → liquidator calls batchLiquidateTroves on troves with ICR=105% → _getTotalsFromBatchLiquidate_NormalMode uses new MCR → troves liquidated that were previously safe), violating INVARIANT (troves above original MCR must not be liquidatable without a price change), causing scoped impact: liquidator seizes collateral gas compensation from troves that were solvent under original parameters, extracting BNB at borrower expense? Local proof idea: unit test with MCR change, assert no trove with ICR >= original MCR is liquidated after parameter change alone.

[File: 'File Name: src/SystemState.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: setCCR / _checkRecoveryMode / openTrove] Can an unprivileged BORROWER under PRECONDITIONS (CCR lowered via timelock from 150% to 111%, system TCR=130% which was previously in recovery mode but now is not) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (timelock executes setCCR(1.11e18) → _checkRecoveryMode returns false → borrower calls openTrove with ICR=112% and isDebtIncrease=true → borrowing fee triggered → new TCR check uses new CCR → borrower mints USDS that would have been blocked under original CCR), violating INVARIANT (recovery mode must engage at CCR=150% to protect system solvency), causing scoped impact: borrower extracts USDS debt at ICR that would have been blocked, pushing system toward insolvency? Local proof idea: invariant test asserting that if TCR < original CCR, no new USDS can be minted without improving ICR above original CCR.

[File: 'File Name: src/SystemState.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: setMinNetDebt / _requireAtLeastMinNetDebt / _redeemCollateralFromTrove] Can an unprivileged REDEEMER under PRECONDITIONS (minNetDebt decreased via timelock from 90e18 to 1e18, partial redemption hint computed for newNetDebt=50e18 which was previously below minimum) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (timelock executes setMinNetDebt(1e18) → redeemer calls redeemCollateral with hint for partial redemption leaving newNetDebt=50e18 → _redeemCollateralFromTrove checks _getNetDebt(v.newDebt) < v.minNetDebt using new minNetDebt=1e18 → partial redemption proceeds), violating INVARIANT (partial redemption must not leave trove with net debt below the minimum that was enforced at open), causing scoped impact: redeemer partially redeems leaving a dust trove that cannot be properly managed, enabling redistribution precision loss or future over-redemption? Local proof idea: fuzz test minNetDebt values, assert partial redemption never leaves trove with net debt below original minNetDebt.

[File: 'File Name: src/SystemState.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: setUSDSGasCompensation / _requireValidUSDSRepayment / repayUSDS] Can an unprivileged BORROWER under PRECONDITIONS (trove opened with USDSGasCompensation=10e18 so compositeDebt=netDebt+10e18, timelock decreases USDSGasCompensation to 1e18) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (openTrove → timelock setUSDSGasCompensation(1e18) → borrower calls repayUSDS with amount=debt-1e18 → _requireValidUSDSRepayment checks debtRepayment <= currentDebt.sub(1e18) → borrower repays more than netDebt, leaving only 1e18 in trove), violating INVARIANT (repayment must not reduce trove debt below the gas compensation buffer that was minted), causing scoped impact: borrower repays excess USDS reducing trove debt below original gas compensation, creating accounting mismatch where gas pool holds more USDS than the trove's remaining gas compensation? Local proof idea: unit test repayUSDS after USDSGasCompensation decrease, assert trove.debt >= new USDSGasCompensation after repayment.

[File: 'File Name: src/SystemState.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: setUSDSGasCompensation / getCappedOffsetVals / _liquidateRecoveryMode] Can an unprivileged LIQUIDATOR under PRECONDITIONS (trove opened with USDSGasCompensation=10e18, timelock increases USDSGasCompensation to 20e18, trove ICR between MCR and TCR in recovery mode) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (openTrove → timelock setUSDSGasCompensation(20e18) → liquidator triggers recovery mode liquidation → getCappedOffsetVals uses new USDSGasCompensation=20e18 → collGasCompensation computed on cappedCollPortion → USDSGasCompensation=20e18 sent to liquidator from GasPool which only holds 10e18), violating INVARIANT (USDSGasCompensation sent to liquidator must equal amount held in GasPool for that trove), causing scoped impact: liquidator receives 20e18 USDS but GasPool only has 10e18, draining USDS from other protocol participants? Local proof idea: unit test recovery mode liquidation after USDSGasCompensation increase, assert GasPool balance >= USDSGasCompensation paid out.

[File: 'File Name: src/SystemState.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: setBorrowingFeeFloor / _requireValidMaxFeePercentage] Can an unprivileged BORROWER under PRECONDITIONS (borrowingFeeFloor set to value > DECIMAL_PRECISION=1e18 via timelock, no upper bound enforced in _setBorrowingFeeFloor) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (timelock executes setBorrowingFeeFloor(2e18) → all calls to openTrove/withdrawUSDS in normal mode revert because _requireValidMaxFeePercentage requires maxFeePercentage >= 2e18 but also <= 1e18), violating INVARIANT (borrowingFeeFloor must never exceed DECIMAL_PRECISION or all borrowing is permanently blocked), causing scoped impact: while this is primarily DoS, if attacker front-runs with a large borrow before the parameter change, they extract USDS at old fee while blocking all future borrowers? Local proof idea: unit test with borrowingFeeFloor=2e18, assert openTrove reverts, then assert attacker's pre-change borrow succeeded.

[File: 'File Name: src/SystemState.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: setRedemptionFeeFloor / requireValidMaxFeePercentage] Can an unprivileged REDEEMER under PRECONDITIONS (redemptionFeeFloor set to value > DECIMAL_PRECISION via timelock, no upper bound enforced in _setRedemptionFeeFloor) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (timelock executes setRedemptionFeeFloor(2e18) → all redeemCollateral calls revert because requireValidMaxFeePercentage requires maxFeePercentage >= 2e18 but also <= 1e18), violating INVARIANT (redemptionFeeFloor must never exceed DECIMAL_PRECISION or all redemptions are permanently blocked), causing scoped impact: attacker who redeems before the parameter change extracts BNB at old fee rate while blocking all future redemptions, trapping USDS holders? Local proof idea: unit test with redemptionFeeFloor=2e18, assert redeemCollateral reverts, verify attacker's pre-change redemption succeeded.

[File: 'File Name: src/SystemState.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: setMCR / isValidFirstRedemptionHint / redeemCollateral] Can an unprivileged REDEEMER under PRECONDITIONS (MCR lowered via timelock from 110% to 101%, first redemption hint points to trove with ICR=105% which was previously below MCR and ineligible) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (timelock executes setMCR(1.01e18) → redeemer calls redeemCollateral with firstRedemptionHint pointing to ICR=105% trove → isValidFirstRedemptionHint returns true using new MCR → redemption proceeds against trove that was previously ineligible), violating INVARIANT (redemption must only target troves with ICR >= MCR at the time of redemption, using consistent MCR), causing scoped impact: redeemer extracts BNB from troves that were previously protected from redemption, at the expense of those trove owners? Local proof idea: unit test with MCR change, assert redemption hint validity is consistent before and after MCR change.

[File: 'File Name: src/SystemState.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: setMCR / requireTCRoverMCR / redeemCollateral] Can an unprivileged REDEEMER under PRECONDITIONS (MCR lowered via timelock, system TCR was previously below MCR blocking redemptions, now TCR >= new MCR) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (timelock executes setMCR(newLowerMCR) → requireTCRoverMCR passes with new MCR → redeemer calls redeemCollateral → extracts BNB that was previously locked due to TCR < old MCR), violating INVARIANT (redemptions must be blocked when system is undercollateralized relative to the original MCR), causing scoped impact: redeemer extracts BNB from an undercollateralized system, worsening insolvency and harming remaining depositors? Local proof idea: unit test with TCR between new and old MCR, assert redeemCollateral is blocked under original MCR but passes under new MCR.

[File: 'File Name: src/SystemState.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: setUSDSGasCompensation / _getNetDebt / _requireAtLeastMinNetDebt] Can an unprivileged BORROWER under PRECONDITIONS (USDSGasCompensation increased via timelock from 10e18 to 50e18, existing trove has debt=100e18) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (openTrove with debt=100e18 → timelock setUSDSGasCompensation(50e18) → borrower calls repayUSDS → _requireAtLeastMinNetDebt checks _getNetDebt(newDebt) = newDebt.sub(50e18) → if newDebt < 50e18 after repayment, underflow reverts), violating INVARIANT (_getNetDebt must not underflow for any valid trove after parameter change), causing scoped impact: borrower's trove becomes permanently uncloseable and unrepayable, trapping collateral and creating bad debt? Local proof idea: fuzz test USDSGasCompensation values against existing trove debt values, assert _getNetDebt never underflows.

[File: 'File Name: src/SystemState.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: setConfigs / _setMCR / _setCCR ordering] Can an unprivileged OWNER under PRECONDITIONS (setConfigs called with _mcr and _ccr where _ccr is set first using current mcr=0, then _mcr is set using new ccr) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (owner calls setConfigs with _ccr=1.1e18 and _mcr=1.09e18 → _setCCR(1.1e18) passes since 1.1e18 > mcr=0 → _setMCR(1.09e18) passes since 1.09e18 > 1e18 && 1.09e18 < 1.1e18 → MCR=109% CCR=110%), violating INVARIANT (CCR must be meaningfully above MCR to provide a safety buffer for recovery mode), causing scoped impact: with CCR only 1% above MCR, recovery mode provides no meaningful protection, allowing borrowers to extract USDS at near-MCR collateral ratios without triggering recovery mode? Local proof idea: unit test setConfigs with MCR=109% CCR=110%, assert recovery mode buffer is insufficient for protocol safety.

[File: 'File Name: src/SystemState.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: setMCR / _setMCR / _setCCR interaction] Can an unprivileged TIMELOCK EXECUTOR under PRECONDITIONS (MCR=110% CCR=150%, timelock first calls setCCR(111%) then setMCR(110.5%)) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (timelock executes setCCR(1.11e18) → _setCCR checks 1.11e18 > mcr=1.1e18 ✓ → timelock executes setMCR(1.105e18) → _setMCR checks 1.105e18 > 1e18 && 1.105e18 < 1.11e18 ✓ → MCR=110.5% CCR=111%), violating INVARIANT (CCR must remain significantly above MCR to prevent recovery mode from being triggered by normal market fluctuations), causing scoped impact: with CCR-MCR gap of only 0.5%, any minor price drop triggers recovery mode, allowing recovery mode borrowing at ICR=111% to extract USDS while system is technically in recovery? Local proof idea: unit test with MCR=110.5% CCR=111%, assert recovery mode triggers at TCR=111.5% and borrowing at ICR=111% is allowed.

[File: 'File Name: src/SystemState.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: setUSDSGasCompensation / openTrove / closeTrove accounting] Can an unprivileged BORROWER under PRECONDITIONS (USDSGasCompensation=10e18 at open, decreased to 0 via timelock before close) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (openTrove mints 10e18 to GasPool and records compositeDebt=netDebt+10e18 → timelock setUSDSGasCompensation(0) → closeTrove: user burns debt.sub(0)=compositeDebt, gasPool burns 0 → activePool.decreaseUSDSDebt(compositeDebt) twice effectively), violating INVARIANT (sum of USDS burned on close must equal sum minted on open, and activePool.getUSDSDebt() must decrease by exactly compositeDebt), causing scoped impact: USDS supply/debt accounting permanently desynchronized, GasPool holds 10e18 USDS that can be claimed by future liquidators as unearned gas compensation? Local proof idea: invariant test asserting usdsToken.totalSupply() == sum of all trove composite debts + GasPool balance after every close.

[File: 'File Name: src/SystemState.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: setMinNetDebt / _requireAtLeastMinNetDebt / openTrove] Can an unprivileged BORROWER under PRECONDITIONS (minNetDebt increased via timelock from 90e18 to 500e18 after many troves opened with netDebt=100e18) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (multiple openTroves with netDebt=100e18 → timelock setMinNetDebt(500e18) → borrower calls adjustTrove to repay debt → _requireAtLeastMinNetDebt checks _getNetDebt(newDebt) >= 500e18 → repayment blocked if it would leave netDebt < 500e18), violating INVARIANT (existing troves must be able to repay debt regardless of minNetDebt changes), causing scoped impact: borrowers cannot repay debt to reduce risk, troves become stuck above minimum and cannot be closed normally, forcing liquidation and collateral loss? Local proof idea: unit test with minNetDebt increase, assert repayUSDS is not blocked for existing troves below new minimum.

[File: 'File Name: src/SystemState.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: setCCR / _checkRecoveryMode / _requireValidAdjustmentInCurrentMode] Can an unprivileged BORROWER under PRECONDITIONS (CCR increased via timelock from 150% to 200%, system TCR=160% which was previously normal mode but now is recovery mode) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (timelock executes setCCR(2e18) → _checkRecoveryMode returns true since TCR=160% < CCR=200% → borrower calls adjustTrove with isDebtIncrease=true → _requireValidAdjustmentInCurrentMode in recovery mode requires ICR >= CCR=200% and newICR >= oldICR → borrower must provide ICR=200% to borrow), violating INVARIANT (CCR increase must not retroactively trap existing borrowers in recovery mode constraints), causing scoped impact: borrowers who were in normal mode cannot adjust troves without meeting new CCR, and if they cannot meet it, their troves are stuck, potentially leading to forced liquidation? Local proof idea: unit test with CCR increase, assert existing troves can still adjust within original parameters.

[File: 'File Name: src/SystemState.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: setUSDSGasCompensation / _getCompositeDebt / ICR calculation] Can an unprivileged BORROWER under PRECONDITIONS (USDSGasCompensation increased via timelock from 10e18 to 100e18, existing troves have compositeDebt=netDebt+10e18 stored) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (openTrove with compositeDebt=netDebt+10e18 stored in Troves[borrower].debt → timelock setUSDSGasCompensation(100e18) → getCurrentICR uses stored debt=netDebt+10e18 → ICR appears higher than it should be with new gas comp), violating INVARIANT (ICR calculation must reflect the true composite debt including current gas compensation), causing scoped impact: troves appear more collateralized than they are under new gas compensation, delaying liquidation and creating bad debt when liquidation eventually occurs? Local proof idea: unit test comparing getCurrentICR before and after USDSGasCompensation increase, assert ICR reflects true liability.
